title: Epic
description: Source-of-truth file for an AGF roadmap epic. Stories in `backlog/sprints/*.sprint.json` reference an epic via the `story.epic` field. `HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md` is a generated view over the set of `backlog/epics/*.epic.json` files. Editing the rendered Markdown directly is a `backlog:check` failure.

agfFormatVersion.description: AGF schema version. Bumped only when the epic file shape changes incompatibly.

id.description: Epic id, all-caps kebab. Matches the same regex as story ids. Stable across sprints; story `epic` fields point at this id. minLength:2 keeps legacy short ids (M1, M2…) valid.

title.description: Short human-readable epic title (≤80 chars recommended).

status.description: Epic lifecycle. `planned` = on the roadmap but no sprint has picked stories yet; `active` = at least one sprint has shipped stories or has open stories tagged to it; `done` = all in-scope stories shipped, no follow-up planned; `parked` = explicitly deferred indefinitely (still listed so the agent doesn't re-discover it as a new idea).

category.description: Coarse classification for filtering. `engine` = generic primitives shipped under `engine/`; `sample-game` = specific game project under `examples/<project>/`; `infra` = repo / CI / agent tooling; `research` = investigation-only, no production code.

summary.description: One-paragraph rationale: what is this epic, why does it exist, what scope is in / out.

targetMilestone.description: Optional free-text milestone label, e.g. `MVP-0`, `public-beta`, `S100`. Used by `backlog:epics --milestone <label>` filters and the HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG rollup.

dependsOn.description: Other epic ids that must reach `done` before this epic can leave `planned`. Forms the cross-epic dependency graph; `backlog:check` rejects cycles.

sourceDoc.description: Optional pointer to the design / research doc this epic was distilled from, e.g. `notes/DynaBomber.md` or `docs/research/m21-webgpu-spike.md`.

doneCriteria.description: Human-checkable bullets that define `done` for this epic. Filled out when the epic is first authored, so future agents can decide whether status:"done" is warranted without re-reading every story.

notes.description: Free-text observations (measurement summaries, links to research notes, design pivots). Behaves like `sprint.notes[]`.
